title: Wholesome Pungent Brazilian Cheese Grill
description: A solo‑dinner Brazilian dish that pairs crumbly cheese and fiery tomato confit on a binchotan grill, finished with a sweet amazake glaze for balanced flavor and anti‑inflammatory benefits.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan grill to high heat (around 200°C) before use.
Cut the cheese into thick slices, about 1.5 cm, and season both sides with salt, black pepper, turmeric, and minced garlic.
In a small bowl, mix olive oil, butter, grated ginger, fresh thyme, and a dash of fresh parsley into a glaze.
Place the cheese slices on the grill and cook until the edges brown and the cheese starts to melt, about 3 minutes per side.
While the cheese is grilling, sear the cherry tomatoes on the hot grill for 1 to 2 minutes until they blister and develop a slight char.
Once the cheese is grilled, brush each slice with the amazake glaze, then sprinkle the confit tomatoes on top.
Transfer the plated cheese and tomato mixture back to the grill for an additional 1 to 2 minutes to meld flavors and melt the glaze.
Remove from grill, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve immediately as a single‑serving dinner.
